_design: Layer 14 — Domain registry with reverse indexes and 3-axis taxonomy. Every entity knows its domain; every domain knows its entities. Zero compression: sub-topics within a domain are preserved as first-class children, not flattened. Three axes per entity: functional (business), module (SAP module code), process (UNESCO process B2R/H2R/P2P/T2R/P2D). Established Session #056.

BCM.description: Bank Communication Management — payment batch orchestration, dual-control workflow (90000003 main, 90000004 BNK_COM, 90000005 BNK_INI), signatory/approver rules stored as PD objects OTYPE='RY' under PLVAR='01', exception-list governance (AE/JO/embargo).

dual_control_gap.description: 3,359 same-user batches 2024-2026 (CRUSR=CHUSR), $656M exposure. Root cause: structural understaffing of HQ treasury (only 2 operators: C_LOPEZ 94.7%, I_MARQUAND 92.9%). Not fraud — automation debt.

signatory_rules.description: Workflow responsibility groups 90000004 BNK_COM / 90000005 BNK_INI stored as PD objects (HRP1000/HRP1001) OTYPE='RY', PLVAR='01'. Every BCM spec for a new office or rule must include both RY STEXT and the OTYPE='RY' PLVAR='01' keys.

workflow_mod_path.description: Path 4 remediation — preventive workflow 90000003 modification to enforce CHUSR != CRUSR at SAP level. Blocked on YWFI package source extraction (H14).

Treasury.description: Treasury operations — house bank configuration, electronic bank statement (EBS) import and posting, payment execution (F110 + BCM), cash management. Integrates 9 company codes, 2 currencies, and 6+ house banks.

companions_status.treasury_operations_companion_v2.html: DORMANT — monitor pages do not work (flagged session #059)

house_bank_configuration.description: 13 config steps for house bank create/modify/close, 15 compliance checks, cross-system D01↔P01 comparison, ECO09 benchmark patterns. Tables: T012 (house bank), T012K (account ID), SKA1/SKB1/SKAT (G/L), T030H (posting keys), T035D (posting string), T018V (reason codes), T042I (bank clearing), TIBAN (IBAN), SETLEAF (hierarchy).

bank_statement_ebs.description: E2E bank statement processing: MT940 import, EBS posting (FEB_BSPROC), clearing, FEBAN reconciliation. 3 config tiers (basic/posting-rules/interpretation algorithms). Volumes: 223K FEBEP (statement items), 964K FEBRE (line items). Process mining: 263K events.

bank_reconciliation.description: Bank reconciliation program inventory + process model. Two tiers: (a) CENTRAL substitution chain YRGGBS00 XREF1/XREF2 (UXR1/UXR2 FORMs) with GB901 prerequisites; (b) FIELD-OFFICE custom Y-stack — 15 programs wrapping standard clearing TCODEs (FB08/F-04/FBRA) via BDC CALL TRANSACTION.

field_office_custom_clearing.description: UNESCO Y-stack of 15 custom bank-reconciliation programs used by FIELD OFFICES (UBO/FU sites like Maputo MZN, Brasilia BRA, Eco09) to orchestrate selection + clearing. Wrap standard FI clearing TCODEs (FB08, F-04, FBRA) via BDC CALL TRANSACTION. NOT a replacement — a thin UNESCO-specific wrapper adding: SKB1 company-code scoping, batch iteration via LDB SAPDBSDF, MZN/ECO/UBA company-code-specific logic, list-output error reporting. Discovered in depth via INC-000006906 (Maputo MZN TIME_OUT, Session #058).
field_office_custom_clearing.bdc_mode_risk: MODE 'E' in CALL TRANSACTION inside LDB loop + slow WAN = cumulative dialog time > rdisp/max_wprun_time = TIME_OUT on field-office SAPGUI. Dormant siblings YTBAE001.abap:118 + YTBAE001_HR.abap:122 carry same defect (C_MOD='E'), kept as latent.
field_office_custom_clearing.anti_patterns_R1_R6: See sap_bank_statement_recon skill section 'UNESCO Custom Recon Programs (Y-Stack)' — 6 R-rules distilled from INC-000006906

central_vs_field_office.central_process: UNES accounts run by HQ treasury — YRGGBS00 substitution + RFEBKA00 EBS + F110 + BCM 90000003 dual-control
central_vs_field_office.field_office_process: UBO/FU accounts WITHIN UNES client but regionally operated — Y-stack recon TCODEs (YTR3) + local dashboards + BDC against FB08/F-04/FBRA

field_offices_known_full_roster.name: Ecobank benchmark
field_offices_known_full_roster.status: reference/benchmark — zero FEBKO rows ever — KU-2026-058-01

field_offices_known_full_roster.name: unknown full name
field_offices_known_full_roster.status: runs without validation/substitution (MGIE/ICBA/IBE noted in MEMORY.md)

field_offices_known_full_roster.name: Jakarta
field_offices_known_full_roster.mentioned_in: xref_office_tagging_model.md — full FOCOD universe 100 codes extracted session #055

dashboards_inventory_2026_04_21.finding: NO unified field-office operations dashboard exists. Knowledge fragmented across 5 layers.
fragmented_sources: incident tickets (INC-000006906 Maputo, INC-000005240 XREF office-tagging)
HTML companions (bcm_dual_control_audit.html, house_bank_configuration_companion.html, payment_bcm_companion.html — lack field-office filtering)
domain reference docs (bank_reconciliation_program_inventory.md, xref_office_tagging_model.md — each isolated)
companions_with_field_office_data: companions/bcm_dual_control_audit.html (796KB — all company codes incl field offices)
companions/payment_bcm_companion.html (680KB — implicit field-office scope via ZLSCH routing)
proactive_monitors_MISSING: No real-time Y-stack program execution monitor (YTR0-3, YFI_BANK1 invocation tracking)
No per-office operational dashboard (Maputo, Brasilia, IIEP, etc. individually)
No email-based field-office operational digest inventoried
likely_external_not_in_repo: Power BI / Tableau dashboards (finance cube by office)
Excel tracker for transport-request management (per office)
dashboards_inventory_2026_04_21.assessment: Each field office maintains IMPLICIT shadows of HQ Y-stack programs (YTBAM002_HR_UBO hardcodes 'UBO'). No centralized execution monitor of these shadows. Incidents bubble up REACTIVELY (J_DAVANE timeout) rather than proactively.

open_known_unknowns.question: Is YTBAI001 (SMARTLINK CMI940 → MT940) still in production? File paths point to /usr/sap/D01/conversion/...; TBTCO has zero runs.
STAD trace for YTR1 / YTR2 / YTR2_HR to confirm zero interactive use before decommission proposal.
Extract YCL_FI_BANK_RECONCILIATION_BL (2023 OOP class backing YFI_BANK1) for autopsy.
YTBAM002_HR_UBO housekeeping — not included by any executable today; TADIR-delete candidate?
MT940 feed status for ECO09/MZ banks — BST01 stopped 2025-04-17, ECO09/UBA01 zero FEBKO rows ever. Structural — Treasury business decision required.

Why are these custom Y-stack programs needed at all vs running standard F-04/FB08/FBRA directly? User hypothesis: they wrap standard but add UNESCO field-office logic (SKB1 scoping, MZN01/ECO09 company-code branches, list-output error capture). Need explicit catalog of what Y-stack adds beyond standard clearing TCODEs — is the added value justified vs the defects it introduces (MODE 'E' TIME_OUT)?
What custom DASHBOARDS do field offices (Maputo MZN, others) maintain to control their operations? Are these SAP transactions, external reports, or shadow tools?

findings_session_059.follow_up_remaining: Ask UNESCO treasury what dashboards they ACTUALLY use day-to-day at each field office (human-in-the-loop inventory, since code search cannot reach SharePoint/email/Excel)

xref_office_tagging.description: Office-code XREF1/XREF2 substitution model — YRGGBS00 overwrites XREF fields with office codes. Session #055 discovered DOUBLE ASYMMETRY: UXR1 has no GB901 prereq + commented-out guard (fires unconditionally); UXR2 has HKONT-in-6-bank-GLs prereq + active guard.

payment_landscape.description: Full payment landscape: F110 run → BCM batch → DMEE file → bank. 1.4M process mining events, 550K cases, 9 company codes. 8 DMEE trees variations.

Treasury.owner_role: Treasury Functional SME + FI Config Team + Field Office Accountants

operational_tiers.central: HQ Treasury operations — YRGGBS00 substitution, F110, BCM dual-control 90000003, Paris
operational_tiers.field_office: Regional accountants (MZN/BRA/ECO/UBA) using Y-stack TCODEs (YTR3, YFI_BANK1) that BDC-wrap standard FB08/F-04/FBRA. Own dashboards (not yet inventoried).

FI.description: Financial Accounting — GL, AP, AR, posting rules, validations/substitutions (GGB0/GGB1), BusA derivation. Bridges to PSM via FMIFIIT.KNBELNR=BKPF.BELNR.

validations_substitutions.description: GGB0 validations + GGB1 substitutions. OB28/OBBH assignment is OPTIONAL at UNESCO — 3 of 9 institutes (MGIE/ICBA/IBE) run without any. YRGGBS00 holds FORM bodies (UXR1/UXR2 etc.) + declarative prereqs in GB901.

gl_master_data.description: G/L account master: SKA1 (chart of accounts), SKB1 (company-code-specific), SKAT (text). Account comparison script detects wrong HBKID/KTOKS/XINTB; UPDATE via RFC_ABAP_INSTALL_AND_RUN.

busa_derivation.description: Business Area derivation logic for Travel postings. Intercompany scenario exposes LHRTSF01.abap:852 only fills GSBER for same-company lines.

HCM.description: Human Capital Management — employee lifecycle, 36 infotypes, payroll, benefits, Fiori apps (Offboarding, Asr Framework), OM, travel integration.

infotypes.description: 36 infotypes used by UNESCO HCM (PA0001-PA9999 range, PD-RY for workflow groups)

payroll.description: Payroll processing — Allos vs Y1 BDC pattern forensics

PSM.description: Public Sector Management / Fund Management — budget, commitments, FM-FI bridge via FMIFIIT, WRTTP filter, project cost recovery.

fm_fi_bridge.description: Bridge via FMIFIIT.KNBELNR=BKPF.BELNR; FM uses FKBTR (not HSL), PERIO (not POPER), FMBELNR (not BELNR)

Payment.description: Payment execution sub-domain of Treasury — F110, DMEE trees, 8 DMEE variations, BCM batch orchestration, 1.4M events process mining.

BusinessPartner.description: Vendor/customer/BP master data — SCSA vendor category, KTOKK → AKONT mapping, intercompany BP scenarios.

Travel.description: Travel management — PRRW posting, PR02 trip create, BusA derivation, intercompany travel advance scenarios, PTRV tables.

Procurement.description: Procurement — PO release, entry sheets, GR. Enhancements analysis document.

Transport_Intelligence.description: SAP Transport Order intelligence — 7,745 transports classified across 21 modules. Pre-import risk analysis, companion generation, module-level taxonomy.

Integration.description: Interface landscape — 239 RFC destinations, 38 systems, 19.4K IDocs. DBCON (SQL Server: TULIP, UNESDIR), .NET Connector (SISTER, HR Workflow, CMT, UBO, Travel, Mouv, Procurement), MuleSoft (PROJECT02), BizTalk (SuccessFactors planned).

Output.description: Output determination — printing, forms, SmartForms, Adobe forms.

PS.description: Project System — WBS elements, project master, FM-PS bridge via PRPS.OBJNR → POSID.

RE-FX.description: Real Estate Flexible — lease contracts, property master, RE-FX specific posting.

Support.description: Cross-cutting incident & maintenance domain — INC tickets that span multiple functional domains, health checks, auth/SU53 issues.

process_map._design: UNESCO end-to-end process chains — each maps to a set of functional domains that participate. Enables cross-domain queries.

module_map._design: SAP module codes used on objects[X].domain today. Kept non-overlapping with functional axis.

session_activation_hints._design: Keyword → domain activation routing used at session start. Triggers auto-load of relevant knowledge_docs, skills, open KUs/DQs.
